rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes using only visible feature names and provided concrete bounds.
Use helper boolean equations to encode range constraints when the contract requires logical evaluation of numeric bounds.
generic_rules: Ensure all expressions in AGREE contracts use only identifiers visible at the current scope level.
Encode range conditions as boolean helper variables to improve clarity and type consistency.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use 'eq <name> : bool = ...' to define boolean helper expressions for range checks.
Guarantees must reference only outputs or visible features, never subcomponent ports directly.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes without intermediate visibility.
Do not use untyped literals or mixed numeric types without explicit type alignment in boolean expressions.
